checks.a_analysis: The output elements are derived directly from the source document. Table captions like 'Rulemaking Administrative Data' and 'Signature Section' are acceptable AI-generated additions for structural clarity. No hallucinated body text or invented paragraphs were found.

Contact details present in the included sections (e.g., KOMO and KXLY addresses, phone numbers) were verified against the source and are accurate. For example, the KXLY phone numbers (509) 324-4000 and (509) 329-4015 on page 45 match the output in reading order 582.

The output explicitly notes that 35 sections were omitted due to a token budget. This results in the complete loss of content from pages 6-8, 10-33, and 35-42 of the source document, including the FCC Fact Sheet, the majority of the NPRM text, and legislative updates for House Bill 213.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source contains content on pages 6-8, 10-33, and 35-42 (FCC documents, HB 213, NMAC info).

checks.a_analysis: The tables that were included in the output maintain row-count fidelity. The agenda table on page 1, attendees tables on page 2, and invoice/contract tables on pages 43 and 45 all have the correct number of data rows compared to the source.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Aside from the omitted pages, text within included sections is also truncated. In Section 9 (Introduction to NPRM), the first paragraph is cut off mid-sentence after 'Section 902 of the new legislation requires the', losing the remainder of the introductory text from page 9 of the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Section 902 of the new legislation requires the Commission to take action to help address the diversion of 911 fees by states and other jurisdictions for purposes unrelated to 911. (p. 9, paragraph 1)
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 902 of the new legislation requires the

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: In the sections that were successfully converted, wording remains faithful to the source. Numbers, dates (e.g., 'March 4, 2021' on p. 34), and legislative references are accurate.

checks.a_analysis: I examined the document for duplicated text blocks. Headers like the organization name ('North Carolina 911 Board' or 'KXLY') appear multiple times, but this reflects their presence on every page of the source document. No unintentional duplication of paragraphs or data was found.

The heading hierarchy is consistent. Section 1 uses H1 and H2. Section 2 follows with H1, H2, H3, and H4 in correct logical order (H1: Organization -> H2: Meeting Title -> H3: Minutes -> H4: Attendees). Subsequent sections use H1 for document titles. No levels are skipped.

Multiple tables in Section 46 (KXLY Invoice) are structurally corrupted. In tables 591, 593, 595, and 598, the label 'Spots:' from the source was incorrectly included as a column header. This caused a column misalignment where data is shifted relative to its correct header (e.g., the spot number '1' appears under the 'Spots:' header, and the channel 'KXLY' appears under the '#' header). Additionally, Table 596 incorrectly places data values into the header row rather than the table body.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 46: Spots: # Ch Day Air Date Air Time Description Start/End Time Length Ad-ID Rate Type (headers for spot detail)

checks.a_analysis: The reading order follows the source document's page sequence and internal layout logically. The transitions between different types of documents (agenda, minutes, bills, invoices) are handled correctly.

The agenda list in the Minutes (Section 2, index 20) was converted to an unordered list, and the item numbers (1, 2, 3) present in the source were removed. This results in a loss of sequential meaning.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2: 1. Chair Opening Remarks... 2. Roll Call...
checks.b_evidence_output: Index 20: list_type: unordered, items: ["Chair Opening Remarks...", "Roll Call..."]

checks.a_analysis: I verified URLs present in the text (e.g., http://sbgi.net/?p=1224). These match the source document text. No broken, missing, or fabricated links were identified in the evaluated portions.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document faithfully replicates the visual organization of the source PDF. The multi-section structure including the agenda, meeting minutes, federal documents, state bills, and technical information sheets is preserved in the correct logical order. Complex layouts like the advertising contracts and invoices at the end of the document are correctly organized into tabular formats that maintain the source's intent. While fonts and exact spacing differ as expected for HTML, the organization of information allows a user to locate content in the same relative positions as the PDF.

All tables from the source PDF are rendered correctly in the HTML output. This includes the attendee lists and voting tables in the meeting minutes (Pages 2-5), the complex multi-row data grids in the KOMO Contract (Pages 43-44), and the KXLY Invoices (Pages 45-46). Row and column alignments are preserved, headers are distinct, and the data is easily readable and understandable for a sighted user.

All meaningful images and diagrams from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes the organizational logos (Page 1), the 'Major NG9-1-1 Program Areas' graphic (Page 37), the 'NC 911 Board NG9-1-1 Migration Process' flow chart (Page 38), the 'NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture' diagram (Page 38), the website analytics graph (Page 41), and the commercial screenshot (Page 41). All images are rendered correctly without breakage.

A thorough review of the rendered HTML confirms that there is no unintended duplication of body content. Sections appear once in their correct sequence corresponding to the PDF pages. The standard repetition of headings in navigation landmarks (TOC) versus body content is present as intended for accessibility, but no paragraphs, tables, or entire sections are duplicated in the document body.
